summary.intent: Implement application-level HTTP/SOCKS5 proxy support for moltis by adding an `upstream_proxy` config field that routes all outbound traffic through a user-specified proxy.
summary.outcome: Full proxy support implemented across providers, tools, OAuth, MCP auth, Slack, and MS Teams. Config field uses `Secret<String>` to protect credentials, with URL redaction in logs. Committed and PR created (#561) with review fixes applied.

repo: Two separate shared HTTP clients exist: `moltis_tools::shared_http_client()` (had proxy plumbing for sandbox) and `moltis_providers::shared_http_client()` (no proxy support at all).
~30+ places created `reqwest::Client::new()` independently across channels, voice providers, OAuth, MCP auth, and memory embeddings.
The workspace denies `unsafe_code` at the workspace level, making `std::env::set_var` unusable.
Workspace uses Rust 2024 edition, which makes `set_var` unsafe regardless.
Serde helpers `deserialize_option_secret` / `serialize_option_secret` already exist for `Secret<String>` fields in config.
`moltis-common` is a dependency of both `moltis-tools` and `moltis-providers`, making it the correct home for shared HTTP client utilities.
The `just lint` / `just test` recipes use `--all-features` which includes CUDA/llama-cpp features that fail to build on macOS — pre-existing issue, not introduced here.

code.finding: Top-level `MoltisConfig` struct is where `upstream_proxy: Option<Secret<String>>` was added with custom serde helpers.

`build_schema_map()` defines the TOML schema for validation; `check_semantic_warnings()` is where URL format warnings are emitted. `ExposeSecret` must be imported to call `.expose_secret()` on `Secret<String>` values.
New module housing `build_http_client(proxy_url)`, `build_default_http_client()`, `init_upstream_proxy(url)`, `upstream_proxy_url()` global accessor, and `redact_proxy_url()` for safe logging.

Gateway startup initializes both shared clients and the global proxy URL. When trusted-network feature is active, tools use the sandbox proxy; providers/channels use the upstream proxy. Non-trusted-network path needs explicit `#[cfg(not(feature))]` block to init tools client.

teloxide-core bundles reqwest 0.11 (with `default-features = false`) internally. `Bot::with_client()` expects a 0.11 `Client`. Cannot inject a 0.12 `Proxy` object into 0.11's `ClientBuilder` — proxy for Telegram requires env var approach.

workflow: When a dependency bundles an older version of reqwest with `default-features = false`, you cannot cross-inject proxy objects between the two major versions — env var fallback is the only safe path.
Global `OnceLock`-based accessors (like `upstream_proxy_url()`) avoid threading config through every plugin/channel constructor without requiring dependency injection refactors.
taplo auto-sorts TOML feature arrays alphabetically on format — unrelated to the change but worth knowing when reviewing diffs.
The `beads` CLI tool is used for issue tracking and claiming work items within the moltis project.

friction: teloxide bundles reqwest 0.11 internally, making it impossible to apply a reqwest 0.12 `Proxy` to Telegram's bot client without unsafe env var manipulation.
Rust 2024 edition + workspace `unsafe_code = "deny"` makes `std::env::set_var` completely unusable, blocking the env var workaround for Telegram.
`just lint` / `just test` with `--all-features` fails on macOS due to CUDA/llama-cpp build dependencies — required running targeted clippy commands per crate.
`moltis-providers` lacked `moltis-common` as a dependency, requiring a new `http_client` module in common rather than reusing the existing `build_http_client` in `moltis-tools`.
open_items: Telegram proxy support is unresolved — deferred until teloxide upgrades to reqwest 0.12, or a separate mechanism (e.g., a dedicated reqwest 0.11 proxy builder via optional dep) is introduced.
Per-channel proxy configuration (different proxy per channel) was explicitly deferred as a follow-up to issue #548.
Voice providers were listed in the original plan but not updated in the implementation — they still use `Client::new()` directly.
Memory embeddings client was listed in the plan but not confirmed as updated in the transcript.
Discord and WhatsApp channel clients were identified as using `Client::new()` but not addressed in the implementation.
